raw_input:
[2.0/5] Skip the "rapid" solution. Claire's is great for ear piercing!

But they aren't a great source for medical advice, especially when they're trying to up-sell you on magic healing liquid!

You can search "American Academy or Dermatology" or a similar site for proper follow-up advice. [1.0/5] Watered down bleach water! I did not buy this via amazon but was sold this at Claire’s as the only option. When using it on my kiddo I could smell the “bleach” smell. I googled the ingredients and sure enough it is watered down bleach. This seems wrong for so many reasons. But basically they are selling you an outrageous bottle of bleach water because it makes them money. [1.0/5] Wish I knew it was bleach Wish I would have known, but after looking into it we decided to make a solution with distilled water and tea tree oil and pink Himalayan salt. No need to be using bleach on my daughters skin. Returned at Claire's. $12 for bleach. Ridiculous. [1.0/5] Does not work as advertised I had my ears pierced at Icing at the local mall. I was told that this solution would have my ears healed in 3 - 4 weeks.
I used the solution faithfully three times each day.
After 4 weeks, I removed the piercing studs and found it very difficult to place new earrings in the holes. They bled and swelled so badly, that I had to wait two weeks for the holes heal and grow closed and have the ears pierced again.
Now I will wait 6 - 8 weeks before removing the new piercing studs.
At $70 to pierce each time (cost of solutions and stainless steel studs) I have lost a lot of money because of a product that does not live up to expectations. [5.0/5] Five Stars Love this stuff. My ear was not healing and used it and my ear healed right up. [1.0/5] Bad idea Claire's upcharged me for this stuff without asking when I had my daughter's ears pierced. After 3 weeks, stupidly, we changed her earrings. Her piercings were NOT healed. I wasn't sure we weren't going to need to repierce them. They bled and scabbed. And she'd been treating them 3 times daily. I didn't realize that the usual wait time is 6 weeks or I would have waited longer. After a traumatic and bloody re-insertion of earrings, we waited another six weeks and then were able to change them without trouble. [4.0/5] Really works, no problem with bleaching clothes I obtained the product from Claire's after getting my ears pierced. Love it. I have found that it also helps other cuts and injuries heal faster as well. I get skin burns from an allergic reaction and I found that those healed quicker too. I liked it so much I am trying to find a way to purchase it outside of getting ears pierced. As far as the comments go about it bleaching out clothes. I have not found this to be the case (at least not where I am concerned). I was not careful and let it get on my clothes and so far I have had no issues. [1.0/5] Yeah, nah. I've had many piercings over the years, several AT Claire's. I've never had a problem until this last set of earlobe piercings where the new 3 week after care was all they sold. Immediately, it smells of bleach. I figured this was just a coincidence and used it twice a day just like I did with saline solution for ALL my other body piercings that I've never had a problem with. A week after I had my ears pierced, they still hurt just as bad as the first day. I called the store and they were super helpful and friendly, so this is definitely not their fault. Finally, today, I looked up the product and realized it had bleach in it. I cannot fathom how this is okay to use. Even if it's a SMALL amount, I can't even begin to understand how this is okay to put on a brand new piercing and also somehow heal it in three weeks. I don't necessarily recommend not getting your ears pierced at Claire's because I've never had a problem with it, but don't use this solution. Just buy saline solution, honestly. Using this product on your ears will not heal them in three weeks. I don't know how other people haven't had problems. [5.0/5] It’s not bleach, so stop saying it is Hey guys, this isn’t bleach. It’s not watered down bleach. It does share ingredients with bleach, but bleach also has many other ingredients. Saying that this product is bleach is like saying that bread is a cake because they share ingredients, it’s just not true. For the record, the main ingredient in both Claire’s ear care solution and bleach (sodium hypochlorite) is also commonly used in swimming pools to clean them. Using this ear care solution is not pouring bleach on an open wound unless you also consider jumping in a pool as bathing in bleach. Also, any piercing specialist would recommend using salt water to clean a new piercing. Sodium hypochlorite (NaClo) is a salt, a salt quite similar to table salt actually (NaCl), so this is basically a salt water solution. Finally, the bleach smell people complain about really isn’t there, this product smells of chlorine, which is in bleach as well as pools as well as many salts. Don’t be fooled by simple chemistry, this product is not bleach. [1.0/5] This is bleach! Google the ingredients. This is watered down bleach, people! It smells like bleach. The bottle says it can "discolor clothing". THAT IS BECAUSE IT IS BLEACH!!!!! Saline water is all that is needed to clean a piercing. So scary that this is sold to us as aftercare. [2.0/5] Whats with the phosphoric acid? Ouch. Smells like bleach. Once I found the ingredients I found that it wasn't. My first mistake was getting her ears pierced somewhere other than a piercer that uses needles and not guns. However, I forgot that this was an option since everything closed after covid struck. In any case, I used this once and my daughter said it burned/stung so I did what ive done for my own piercings and used bottled/distilled water and a little bit of salt. I rinsed this stuff out of her ears with my mixture and she immediately expressed that she felt relieved. She also woke up the next morning saying she felt virtually no pain from the new piercings.

Maybe this works for other people but it smells like bleach and I fail to see why there is phosphoric acid in it. [1.0/5] Solution does contain MAIN bleach ingredients This product does in fact contain the the main ingredient found in bleach- Sodium Hypochlorite. Sodium Hypochlorite is sometimes called ‘liquid bleach’. Claire’s gave this solution to me after I got my ears pierced for the 4th time and while it did not irritate my skin, I chose to just use a saline solution (like the previous times before). It also discolored my shirt but does warn on the bottle this could happen (because it’s bleach) [2.0/5] Claire's Lied I was specifically told the Rapid After Care Cleanser had Neosporin and Saline Solution, which it is not. It is Bleach and Phosphoric Acid. It has worked really well. We started using it again because after the 3 weeks my daughter bumped her ear pretty good and it got infected so I started using it again. The infection is leaving. The product is working. But it's still bleach! That's what I am so shocked at. They lied. And obviously by all this battling there is a problem with their approach (and for my circumstance dishonesty). [4.0/5] Its different for everyone Reading others review id say everyones body just heals differently. For me this product really worked well, i got my cartlidge pierced and i just could not get rid of the infection after 2-3 months of it still being grossly infected and trying everything i could find on google, i went to the doctors and they even told me my body is probably rejecting it and to just take it out. This was my last resort before just closing it up and it worked within a month or less. My piercing is completely healed now and i got my second one done and using this consistently has prevented it from the horrible infection bump the first time [5.0/5] No, it's Not "bleach". Sodium Hypochlorite is commonly used in wound care No,Sodium Hypochlorite is not bleach! It can be used as a bleaching agent and is COMMON in wound care. Even if it shares ingredients. It's basically a saline solution with phosphoric acid added. It's totally safe. If you go to a tattoo shop and get a piercing, you are told to clean with salt water. That's basically what this is, with added Phosphoric acid. And the only thing the phosphoric acid does is balance your Ph. People calling this bleach are highly overreacting. I have had very many peircings. (10 total) and have used this on several of them. I got a conch peircing at a tattoo shop and cleaned exactly how they told me. I got a keloid. Couldn't get it to heal. I used this Claire's rapid solution and it healed in less than a week. I love this stuff I think it's amazing. While yes, everyone heals differently, it may not be perfect for everyone, my point is simply that it is safe for most people, and I've had a great experience with it personally. [5.0/5] It works great cut my healing time in half the time Great [5.0/5] Definitely works It absolutely works on every piercing I added to my ear including cartilage

response:
Product get mixed reviews. Here summary:

BAD THINGS PEOPLE SAY:

Many reviewers angry about bleach ingredient - sodium hypochlorite. Smell like bleach, can discolor clothing. Some people say Claire's staff lie about ingredients, claim product contain Neosporin or saline. Several people say product not heal piercing in promised 3-4 weeks. Some kids and adults experience burning, stinging, pain. Multiple reviewers say plain saline solution work better. Product cost $12, seem overpriced for ingredients. Some reviewers say Claire's push product without asking, like upsell trick.

GOOD THINGS PEOPLE SAY:

Some people say product work great, heal piercings fast. One person say healed stubborn cartilage infection after doctor give up. Another say help heal other cuts and skin burns too. Few reviewers defend sodium hypochlorite ingredient, say it common in wound care and swimming pools, not same as household bleach. Some say no bleach smell, no clothing damage.

BOTTOM LINE:

Product divide people. Many angry about ingredients and false healing time claims. Few people love it and say work well. Most critics recommend plain saline solution instead. Healing time vary by person. Claire's upsell tactics annoy many customers.
